intake.business_question: Identify the drivers of customer churn

decisions.detail: Excluded customerID — operational identifier with no analytical value
Excluded TotalCharges — collinear with tenure (r=0.826); MonthlyCharges retained to capture spend independently
TotalCharges 11 null rows moot — column excluded
SeniorCitizen recoded from 0/1 integer to Yes/No string to match all other binary columns
Service columns with 3 levels collapsed to Yes/No — third level captured by InternetService and PhoneService
Retain all 18 variables for driver analysis — let model assign near-zero importance rather than pre-excluding

Top 7 drivers confirmed: Contract_Month-to-month, tenure, MonthlyCharges, InternetService_Fiber optic, InternetService_No, Contract_Two year, PaymentMethod_Electronic check. Ranks 8-38 confirmed as non-drivers.
Fiber optic churn elevated treated as a product problem — not a pricing or demographic artifact. Recommend product/service quality investigation.
Electronic check payment treated as a demographic correlate of uncommitted/new customers — not an actionable intervention target in its own right.

null_flags: TotalCharges: 0.16% null (11 rows) — new customers with tenure=0; column excluded so moot
distribution_flags: tenure: strong negative correlation with churn (-0.352) — churners concentrated in early months
MonthlyCharges: positive correlation with churn (+0.193) — higher spend = more churn

flags_resolved: SeniorCitizen encoding inconsistency — recoded to Yes/No
Service columns 3-level values — collapsed to Yes/No

segmentation.reason: Already have the churn or no churn flag — those are the two segments we are trying to understand

key_differentiators.direction: Churners avg 18mo vs Retained avg 38mo — shorter tenure = higher churn risk
88.6% of churners on month-to-month; two-year holders barely churn (2.8%)
Fiber optic churn 41.9%; DSL 19.0%; No internet 7.4%
Proxy for uncommitted customer profile — collapsed to near-zero in models
Correlated with tenure/contract — not independent driver
Correlated with tenure/contract — not independent driver

segment_narratives.Churned: New, uncommitted, high-spend customers on flexible contracts. Median tenure 10 months; 52.9% of 0-6 month customers churn. Almost exclusively month-to-month (88.6%), skew heavily to Fiber optic (69.4%), pay $74.40/month avg, lack protective add-ons, and are disproportionately single with no dependents.
segment_narratives.Retained: Established, committed customers with multi-year contracts, lower monthly charges ($61.30 avg), automatic payment methods, more add-on services, and longer household commitments.

user_decisions.feature_selection_for_driver_analysis: Retain all 18 variables — let model assign near-zero importance to weak features rather than pre-excluding

flags_resolved: Fiber optic + MonthlyCharges co-movement — resolved: treat as product problem
Electronic check and PaperlessBilling overlap — resolved: both are demographic correlates of uncommitted customers
OnlineSecurity and TechSupport in tandem — resolved: correlated with tenure/contract, not independent drivers

model_quality.quality_assessment: reliable — all three models AUC > 0.83, consistent driver rankings across methods

top_drivers.direction: Month-to-month contract → strongly associated with higher churn
Longer tenure → lower churn risk; first 12 months are critical window
Higher monthly charges → more churn; churners pay $74.4 avg vs $61.3 for retained
Fiber optic → nearly 2x churn odds (OR=1.97); interpreted as product quality problem
No internet service → half the churn odds; phone-only customers have high inertia
Two-year contract → strong churn protection; these customers churn at only 2.8%
Electronic check → demographic correlate of new/uncommitted customers, not an independent intervention target

flags_for_validation.description: Fiber optic churn elevated confirmed as product problem. Recommend investigating Fiber-specific NPS, complaint data, or service quality metrics to identify the specific failure point.
Electronic check confirmed as demographic correlate of uncommitted/new customers. Not an independent actionable signal.

user_decisions.non_drivers_confirmed: ranks 8-38 confirmed as non-drivers — gender, PhoneService, MultipleLines, StreamingTV, StreamingMovies, OnlineBackup, DeviceProtection, Partner, Dependents, SeniorCitizen, TechSupport, OnlineSecurity, non-electronic-check payment methods
user_decisions.fiber_optic_interpretation: product problem — not pricing or demographic artifact
user_decisions.electronic_check_interpretation: demographic correlate — not actionable intervention target

flags_resolved: Fiber optic interpretation confirmed as product problem
Electronic check interpretation confirmed as demographic correlate

recommendations.headline: Launch a contract commitment programme targeting new month-to-month customers in months 1–6
recommendations.motivating_finding: Contract type is #1 driver (score 0.845); 52.9% churn rate in first 6 months; two-year contracts churn at 2.8%

recommendations.headline: Investigate and remediate the Fiber optic product experience
recommendations.target_segment: All Fiber optic customers (3,096 customers, 44% of base)
recommendations.motivating_finding: Fiber optic OR=1.97 in logistic regression; 41.9% churn rate; confirmed product problem by analyst

recommendations.headline: Design a value reinforcement programme for high-charge month-to-month customers in year 1
recommendations.target_segment: Month-to-month customers paying above $70/month, tenure 0–12 months
recommendations.motivating_finding: MonthlyCharges #3 driver (score 0.613); churners pay $13.10/month more than retained customers

open_questions.question: What specifically is failing in the Fiber optic product — reliability, support quality, or feature gaps?
open_questions.data_needed: NPS scores, complaint logs, service interruption records, and technician call rates segmented by churn status

open_questions.question: Is month-to-month churn driven by contract optionality or customer self-selection?
open_questions.data_needed: Randomised test of the contract commitment programme to separate incentive effect from self-selection bias

open_questions.question: Is churn accelerating or decelerating over time? Is the Fiber optic problem recent?
open_questions.data_needed: Temporal dimension — quarterly cohort churn rates over the observation period
